137.reason: [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the code still assigns tokenType = TokenType.AND and proceeds to add a token. This results in both an error and an invalid token being generated, which violates the specification that erroneous inputs should only produce error output and no tokens.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the code still assigns tokenType = TokenType.AND and proceeds to add a token. This results in both an error and an invalid token being generated, which violates the specification that erroneous inputs should only produce error output and no tokens.]

144.reason: [Same issue as above: for a single '|', an error is added, but the lexer still sets tokenType to OR and will output a token, which is incorrect behavior for an erroneous input.]
